message:
feat(core): wire protocol aggregator prunes stale agents before capacity rejection

Previously, when the Aggregator hit its max_agents capacity limit, any new
sender was hard-rejected with TooManyAgents even if existing tracked senders
had gone silent. This caused permanent slot exhaustion in long-running
distributed deployments where agents restart with new identities.

Changes to wire_protocol.rs:
- Added `DEFAULT_AGENT_STALE_AFTER_MS` constant (5 minutes).
- Aggregator now carries a `stale_after_ms` field configurable via the
  new `with_stale_after(max_agents, stale_after_ms)` constructor.
- `ingest_envelope` calls `prune_stale_agents` before rejecting a new
  sender at capacity, evicting any senders whose `last_seen_ms` exceeds
  the stale threshold relative to the incoming envelope timestamp.
- Duplicate envelopes now refresh `last_seen_ms` on the existing session
  so that keepalive-style retransmissions prevent false stale eviction.
- New public `prune_stale_agents(now_ms) -> usize` method for manual
  eviction if callers need explicit control.
- Three new unit tests: stale pruning before capacity reject, retention
  of recent agents, and duplicate-refresh preventing false eviction.

Changes to distributed_streaming_e2e.rs:
- `DistributedBridge` updated with `new_with_capacity_and_stale` factory
  to exercise the stale threshold in integration scenarios.
- Two new E2E tests: `prunes_stale_sender_and_accepts_new_sender_at_capacity`
  verifies the full ingest→storage pipeline admits a second sender after
  stale eviction, and `duplicate_refresh_prevents_false_stale_eviction`
  confirms that duplicate-refreshed liveness blocks premature pruning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
